copilot-spaces.title: List organization Copilot Spaces

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per_page
parameters.description: The number of results per page (max 100).

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results before this cursor.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor, as given in the Link header. If specified, the query only searches for results after this cursor.

copilot-spaces.descriptionHTML:
Lists Copilot Spaces owned by an organization. The authenticated user must have read access to the organization's Copilot Spaces.

Only Spaces that are readable by the authenticated user are returned. This includes public Spaces and internal Spaces if the user is a member of the organization.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:org scope to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens and GitHub App user access tokens must have been granted access to the organization that owns the space. They must also have been granted access to every repository referenced by resources in a space; spaces with inaccessible resources are omitted from the response.

response.description: Example response for listing organization copilot spaces

spaces.name: Team Planning Space
spaces.description: Organization space for team planning and coordination
The list of Copilot Spaces on this page of results.

base_role.description:
The base role that determines default permissions.
- `no_access`: No default access
- `reader`: Default read permissions
- `writer`: Default write permissions (organization spaces only)
- `admin`: Default admin permissions (organization spaces only)

copilot-spaces.title: Create an organization Copilot Space

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

bodyParameters.name: base_role
bodyParameters.description:
The base role that determines default permissions for organization members.

  • no_access: No default access (default)
  • reader: Organization members can read the space
  • writer: Organization members can read and edit the space
  • admin: Organization members have full admin access to the space

childParamsGroups.name: repository_id
childParamsGroups.description: Repository ID for repository or file resources.

copilot-spaces.descriptionHTML:
Creates a new Copilot Space owned by an organization. The authenticated user must have permissions to create spaces in the organization.

Organization members with appropriate permissions can create Copilot Spaces to be shared within their organization.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:org scope to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens and GitHub App user access tokens must have been granted access to the organization that owns the space. They must also have been granted access to every repository referenced by the submitted resources.

bodyParameters.description: Organization space for team planning and coordination
bodyParameters.general_instructions: Help the team with planning and coordination tasks

copilot-spaces.descriptionHTML:
Gets details about a specific Copilot Space owned by an organization. The authenticated user must have read access to the Space.

Internal Spaces require the authenticated user to be a member of the organization or have been granted read permissions.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:org scope to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens and GitHub App user access tokens must have been granted access to the organization that owns the space. They must also have been granted access to every repository referenced by resources in the space.

copilot-spaces.title: Set an organization Copilot Space

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: space_number
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the Copilot Space.

bodyParameters.name: base_role
bodyParameters.description:
The base role that determines default permissions for organization members. Changing this field requires admin permissions.

  • no_access: No default access (default)
  • reader: Organization members can read the space
  • writer: Organization members can read and edit the space
  • admin: Organization members have full admin access to the space

copilot-spaces.descriptionHTML:
Updates a Copilot Space owned by an organization. The authenticated user must have permissions to update spaces in the organization.

Organization members with appropriate permissions can update Copilot Spaces owned by their organization.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:org scope to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens and GitHub App user access tokens must have been granted access to the organization that owns the space. They must also have been granted access to every repository referenced by resources in the space, including any being added or updated.

copilot-spaces.title: Delete an organization Copilot Space

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: space_number
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the Copilot Space.

copilot-spaces.descriptionHTML:
Deletes a Copilot Space owned by an organization. The authenticated user must have permissions to delete spaces in the organization.

Warning: This action is permanent and cannot be undone. Deleting a Copilot Space will remove all associated resources and configurations.

Organization members with appropriate permissions can delete Copilot Spaces owned by their organization.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need both the read:org and repo scopes to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens and GitHub App user access tokens must have been granted access to the organization that owns the space. They must also have been granted access to every repository referenced by resources in the space.

response.description: The Copilot Space has been successfully deleted.

statusCodes.description: The Copilot Space has been successfully deleted.
Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

parameters.name: username

copilot-spaces.descriptionHTML:
Lists Copilot Spaces owned by a user. The authenticated user must have read access to the user's Copilot Spaces.

Only Spaces that are readable by the authenticated user are returned. This includes the user's own spaces, and public user spaces when accessing another user's spaces.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:user scope to use this endpoint.

copilot-spaces.descriptionHTML:
Creates a new Copilot Space owned by a user. Only the authenticated user can create spaces for their own account.

Users can create personal Copilot Spaces for their individual use.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:user scope to use this endpoint.

copilot-spaces.descriptionHTML:
Deletes a Copilot Space owned by a user. The authenticated user must be the owner of the space.

collaborators.title: List collaborators for an organization Copilot Space

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: space_number
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the Copilot Space.

collaborators.descriptionHTML:
Lists all collaborators for a specific Copilot Space owned by an organization. The authenticated user must have appropriate permissions to view collaborators.

Each collaborator entry specifies which user or team has access to the space and at what level (reader, writer, or admin). The space owner (organization) is excluded from this list.

Note: Team collaborators listed here are teams that are defined in the organization.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:org scope to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens and GitHub App user access tokens must have been granted access to the organization that owns the space. They must also have been granted access to every repository referenced by resources in the space.

collaborators.description: The list of collaborators for this Copilot Space.

items.title: Copilot Space Collaborator
items.description: A collaborator (user or team) of a Copilot Space

role.description: The role granted to the collaborator
The role granted to the collaborator

collaborators.title: Add a collaborator to an organization Copilot Space

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: space_number
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the Copilot Space.

bodyParameters.name: actor_identifier
bodyParameters.description: The username (for users) or team slug (for teams). The numeric ID of a user or team is also accepted.

bodyParameters.name: role
bodyParameters.description: The role to grant to the collaborator.

collaborators.descriptionHTML:
Adds a collaborator (user or team) to a specific Copilot Space owned by an organization. The authenticated user must have appropriate permissions to manage collaborators.

Note: When adding users as collaborators, they must already be members of the organization. When adding teams as collaborators, they must be defined in the organization.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the write:org scope to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens and GitHub App user access tokens must have been granted access to the organization that owns the space. They must also have been granted access to every repository referenced by resources in the space.

schema.description: A collaborator (user or team) of a Copilot Space

bodyParameters.description: The new role to grant to the collaborator. Use no_access to remove the collaborator.

Updates the role of a collaborator for a specific Copilot Space owned by an organization. The authenticated user must have appropriate permissions to manage collaborators.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the write:org scope to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens and GitHub App user access tokens must have been granted access to the organization that owns the space. They must also have been granted access to every repository referenced by resources in the space.

Lists all collaborators for a specific Copilot Space owned by a user. The authenticated user must be the owner of the space or have admin access to the space.

schema.description: A collaborator (user or team) of a Copilot Space

role.description: The role granted to the collaborator
The role granted to the collaborator

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

collaborators.title: Set a collaborator role for a Copilot Space for a user

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

parameters.name: space_number
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the Copilot Space.

parameters.name: actor_type
parameters.description: The type of actor (must be User for user-owned spaces; Team will be rejected).

parameters.name: actor_identifier
parameters.description: The username of the collaborator. The numeric user ID is also accepted.

bodyParameters.name: role
bodyParameters.description: The new role to grant to the collaborator. Use no_access to remove the collaborator.

collaborators.descriptionHTML:
Updates the role of a collaborator for a specific Copilot Space owned by a user. The authenticated user must be the owner of the space or have admin access to the space.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the user scope to use this endpoint.

schema.title: Copilot Space Collaborator
schema.description: A collaborator (user or team) of a Copilot Space

role.description: The role granted to the collaborator
The role granted to the collaborator

statusCodes.description: Response when role is no_access and the collaborator was removed.
Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

collaborators.title: Remove a collaborator from a Copilot Space for a user

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The handle for the GitHub user account.

parameters.name: space_number
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the Copilot Space.

parameters.name: actor_type
parameters.description: The type of actor (must be User for user-owned spaces; Team will be rejected).

parameters.name: actor_identifier
parameters.description: The username of the collaborator. The numeric user ID is also accepted.

collaborators.descriptionHTML:
Removes a collaborator from a specific Copilot Space owned by a user. The authenticated user must be the owner of the space or have admin access to the space.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the user scope to use this endpoint.

resources.title: List resources for an organization Copilot Space

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: space_number
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the Copilot Space.

resources.descriptionHTML:
Lists all resources attached to a specific Copilot Space owned by an organization. The authenticated user must have appropriate permissions to view the space.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:org scope to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens and GitHub App user access tokens must have been granted access to the organization that owns the space. They must also have been granted access to every repository referenced by resources in the space.

resources.description: The list of resources attached to this Copilot Space.

items.title: Copilot Space Resource
items.description: A resource attached to a Copilot Space.

id.description: The unique identifier of the resource.

copilot_chat_attachment_id.description: The ID of the associated chat attachment, if any.

metadata.description: Resource-specific metadata. The keys and values depend on the resource type.

created_at.description: The date and time the resource was created.

updated_at.description: The date and time the resource was last updated.

resources.title: Create a resource for an organization Copilot Space

parameters.name: org
parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: space_number
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the Copilot Space.

resources.descriptionHTML:
Creates a new resource in a specific Copilot Space owned by an organization. The authenticated user must have write permissions on the space.

The following resource types are supported: repository, github_file, free_text, github_issue, github_pull_request. The uploaded_text_file and media_content types are not supported via this endpoint.

For github_file resources, if a resource with the same repository, file path, and SHA already exists, the existing resource is returned with a 200 status.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the write:org scope to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens and GitHub App user access tokens must have been granted access to the organization that owns the space. They must also have been granted access to every repository referenced by resources in the space, including the resource being created.

statusCodes.description: Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed.

parameters.description: The organization name. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: space_number
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the Copilot Space.

parameters.name: space_resource_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the resource.

resources.descriptionHTML:
Gets a specific resource attached to a Copilot Space owned by an organization. The authenticated user must have appropriate permissions to view the space.

OAuth app tokens and personal access tokens (classic) need the read:org scope to use this endpoint.

Fine-grained tokens and GitHub App user access tokens must have been granted access to the organization that owns the space. They must also have been granted access to every repository referenced by resources in the space.
